message:
feat(demo_showcase): implement live theme switching (bd-k52c)

Add runtime theme switching capability:
- set_theme(preset): Instantly changes theme across app
- cycle_theme(): Cycles through Dark -> Light -> Dracula -> Dark
- 't' keyboard shortcut cycles themes globally
- AppMsg::SetTheme and AppMsg::CycleTheme messages

Theme changes are immediate - all views use new colors on next render.
Config.theme is updated to track the current preset.

6 tests cover theme switching, cycling, and config synchronization.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3nrc","title":"E2E: CLI script wrapper for CI + local debugging","description":"Add a human-friendly script that runs the *smoke* E2E suite with good logs.

Deliverables:
- `scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh` (or similar) that:
  - sets deterministic env (seed, no-color optional)
  - runs `cargo test -p demo_showcase` with clear output
  - on failure, prints where artifacts were written

Notes:
- The full scenario suite is added by a follow-up task so we get early value.

Acceptance:
- Running the script locally gives a fast, reliable signal and high-quality failure output.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:40.876265868Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:56.036634053Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","script","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nrc","depends_on_id":"bd-299e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:41.305600278Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3nrc","depends_on_id":"bd-4d5e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:37.442689140Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3nrc","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:40.895165789Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3o6n","title":"QA and Stabilization Session: wish, lipgloss, glamour, huh","description":"Completed a deep dive review and fix cycle for the codebase.

Fixed:
- wish: Output flushing, bubbletea integration, input handling.
- lipgloss: Theme initialization bugs, missing error types.
- glamour: Table rendering (replaced naive implementation with table.rs), updated tests.
- huh: Doc examples, missing helper methods for value extraction.
- charmed_log: Date math (migrated to chrono).

Verified:
- All crates compile cleanly.
- Tests pass for all modified crates.
- Workspace check passes.

Closed related epics/tasks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:46:22.301048189Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:46:29.525183754Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T23:46:29.525106669Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3pm6","title":"demo_showcase data: action API (start job/deploy, emit logs/toasts)","description":"Create a clean *domain action API* so multiple UI surfaces can trigger the same behaviors without depending on each other.

Rationale:
- Currently Jobs page actions and Wizard completion both need to start jobs/deployments.
- If Wizard depends on Jobs page code (or vice versa), the architecture becomes tangled.

Deliverables:
- A set of pure-ish functions (or a small service struct) that:
  - create/start/cancel/retry jobs
  - start deployments / rollbacks
  - emit structured log entries
  - emit user-facing notifications (as app messages)
- The action layer should accept the current domain state + inputs and return:
  - updated state
  - side effects expressed as bubbletea `Cmd` / `AsyncCmd` (feature-gated)
  - events/messages for UI (toasts, navigation)

Implementation guidance:
- Prefer composing effects with `bubbletea::batch(...)` (toast + log + state refresh) instead of ad-hoc "do one thing" commands.
- Use `bubbletea::sequence(...)` for any multi-step workflow that must occur in order (e.g., release-terminal -> run -> restore, or staged deploy steps).

Acceptance:
- Jobs page and Wizard both call this API (no cross-dependencies between pages).
- The API is unit-testable (given state + input -> expected state/events).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:09.840599209Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:13:41.700985132Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:13:41.700919670Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3pm6","depends_on_id":"bd-278q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:09.860556382Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pm6","depends_on_id":"bd-2hwq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:28.941199473Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3ps4","title":"Interactions: enable mouse mode + click/scroll mapping","description":"Enable mouse support in `demo_showcase` and map mouse events to a coherent UX (scroll, click-to-focus/select).

Why:
- Mouse support is a premium signal and makes the showcase feel like a real product.
- It also validates bubbletea’s mouse plumbing (`MouseMsg` parsing + crossterm integration).

Implementation requirements:
- Program configuration:
  - run with mouse enabled (prefer `with_mouse_cell_motion()` to support hover/cell-motion where useful).
  - ensure mouse can be disabled via Settings/Config toggle.
- Scroll mapping:
  - wheel scroll should apply to the *focused pane* (viewport, list, table, file preview).
  - when nothing is explicitly focused, default to the primary content pane.
- Click mapping:
  - click selects within lists/tables/filepicker when coordinates hit rows.
  - click on dashboard cards triggers selection/drill-down.
  - click-to-focus inputs (search bars, text inputs, etc.).

Behavioral constraints:
- Mouse must not fight keyboard navigation:
  - keyboard remains first-class, mouse is additive.
  - selection/focus updates should be consistent regardless of input source.
- Headless/CI safety:
  - mouse support should be a no-op when renderer/terminal features are unavailable.

Acceptance:
- Mouse scroll feels natural in viewports/lists/tables/file preview.
- Clicking to select/focus works in the major screens.
- Disabling mouse immediately stops mouse-driven changes.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - given `MouseMsg` wheel events, verify the correct component state changes (scroll offset, selection).
  - click hit-testing logic for at least one list/table and one card grid.
- E2E (optional but preferred):
  - use the harness “raw byte” mode to inject xterm mouse sequences and assert state changes in artifacts.

Notes:
- Keep hit-testing deterministic: base calculations on known layout metrics (width/height + fixed styles).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:32.064610623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:18:49.256919092Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","input","mouse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ps4","depends_on_id":"bd-114p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:32.093132249Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ps4","depends_on_id":"bd-28jp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:56.981806786Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ps4","depends_on_id":"bd-2tl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:55.722943138Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3q7p","title":"Animations: build a small animation subsystem (springs + tick)","description":"Create a reusable way to animate values over time.

Implementation idea:
- Use harmonica `Spring` to ease values toward targets.
- Drive updates via bubbletea tick/every (or async tick).
- Keep animations deterministic and bounded.

Acceptance:
- Any widget/page can request an animated value update without bespoke code.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:54.209871764Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:27:17.722924637Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:27:17.722852563Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["anim","demo_showcase","harmonica"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3q7p","depends_on_id":"bd-2fnc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:54.244746558Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3q7p","depends_on_id":"bd-2szb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:04.402219642Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3q7p","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:06.871476743Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3q7p","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:05.620584063Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3rwl","title":"Jobs: implement jobs table/list + selection","description":"Implement the main Jobs/Tasks page: a dense, usable jobs table/list with selection.

Why:
- This is the core "ops console" workflow: see what’s running, what failed, and drill into details.
- It showcases Bubbles components (table/list, paginator, progress, spinner) working together.

Implementation options:
- Use `bubbles::table` for a dense table view, or
- Use `bubbles::list` for a richer list with descriptions.

Data to show (minimum):
- job name / id
- status (running/succeeded/failed/cancelled)
- progress (when applicable)
- duration / started-at
- owner/service/environment (as fits the domain model)

Interactions:
- Keyboard navigation and selection.
- Selection updates a details pane (separate task) and enables actions (start/cancel/retry).

Acceptance:
- Keyboard navigation works and feels consistent.
- Selection is stable across refreshes and filtering changes (best-effort).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:18.314504691Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:58:13.418655253Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:58:13.418589240Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","demo_showcase","jobs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3rwl","depends_on_id":"bd-1dr6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:18.330182628Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rwl","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:31.091452200Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rwl","depends_on_id":"bd-3bz7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:35.920756563Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rwl","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:33.415669784Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-gyzr","title":"Code Quality & Reliability Hardening: Deep Review Findings","description":"# Epic: Code Quality & Reliability Hardening

## Origin
This epic captures all deficiencies identified during a comprehensive deep-dive code review
of the entire charmed_rust codebase conducted on 2026-01-27. The review examined all core
crates (bubbletea, lipgloss, bubbles, glamour, huh, charmed_log, harmonica) for:

- Bugs and correctness issues
- Thread safety and concurrency problems
- Performance inefficiencies
- API design issues
- Code duplication and maintainability

## Scope
The findings span multiple crates with varying severity:
- **CRITICAL**: Issues that can cause panics, data loss, or security problems
- **HIGH**: Issues affecting correctness or significant performance
- **MEDIUM**: Code quality, maintainability, and minor performance issues

## Goals
1. Eliminate all sources of unexpected panics (lock poisoning, bounds checks)
2. Ensure reliable I/O with proper error handling
3. Consolidate duplicated implementations for maintainability
4. Optimize hot paths identified during review
5. Fix all identified bugs and edge cases

## Dependencies
This epic's tasks should be completed before major feature work to ensure a stable foundation.

## Quality Bar
Each fix must:
- Include regression tests where applicable
- Pass all existing tests
- Pass clippy with -D warnings
- Be reviewed for thread safety implications","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:14:47.042212490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:14:47.062139404Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["code-quality","hardening","review-findings"]}
 {"id":"bd-i0i9","title":"Docs: docs navigation (list of pages + preview)","description":"Implement a docs index + navigation experience.

Design:
- Split view:
  - Left pane: doc page list (filterable list)
  - Right pane: rendered content (glamour + viewport)

Behavior:
- Switching docs is instant.
- Preserve per-doc scroll position when switching away and back (optional but high polish).
- The list should reflect the authored docs content (title/subtitle) rather than raw filenames.

Integration:
- Hooks into in-doc search (search operates within the current doc).

Acceptance:
- Navigation feels fast and "real product" quality.
- Selection and (optionally) scroll state are preserved across page navigations.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:43.361710616Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:11:29.373781335Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs","list"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-i0i9","depends_on_id":"bd-22vc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:43.387706158Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-i0i9","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:26.023997036Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-iwbw","title":"Dashboard: animated counters/progress (harmonica)","description":"Make the Dashboard feel alive with tasteful, physics-based motion (harmonica).

Why:
- Smooth motion is a signature Charm aesthetic.
- Animated counters and progress bars make metrics changes readable and satisfying.

What to animate:
- Counter values (requests/sec, latency, error rate):
  - spring toward target to avoid jitter
- Progress bars:
  - ease changes rather than snapping
- Optional:
  - a subtle transition when switching focus between dashboard cards

Constraints:
- Must not interfere with input responsiveness.
- Must respect reduce-motion / `--no-animations`:
  - values snap directly to targets

Acceptance:
- Motion is smooth, stable, and never causes flicker.
- The dashboard remains readable even with rapid metric changes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:59.093043228Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:31:09.317467932Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","demo_showcase","harmonica"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iwbw","depends_on_id":"bd-2myg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:19.483793101Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-iwbw","depends_on_id":"bd-3q7p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:20.705038373Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-iwbw","depends_on_id":"bd-dkff","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:59.109088230Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-lxky","title":"Unit tests: animation subsystem determinism + reduce-motion","description":"Add deterministic unit tests for the demo_showcase animation subsystem.

Why:
- The showcase will lean heavily on motion; we need confidence it won’t become flaky or timing-dependent.
- CI must be able to validate animation code paths without real sleeps.

Test coverage:
- Given a fixed dt (e.g., 16ms) and fixed initial/target values, stepping the animation yields deterministic values.
- Animations stay bounded (no NaNs, no runaway values, no panics on extreme dt).
- Reduce-motion / `--no-animations` behavior:
  - animated values immediately snap to target (or the subsystem becomes a no-op) according to the design.
  - no tick scheduling when animations are disabled.

Acceptance:
- Tests do not depend on wall-clock time.
- Failures clearly report which animation invariant broke.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.665155320Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.688983950Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["anim","demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lxky","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.688967780Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lxky","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.679805544Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lxky","depends_on_id":"bd-3q7p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.684774144Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-miqo","title":"E2E scenario: docs render + search + resize reflow","description":"Scenario: docs page rendering quality.

Steps:
- Open Docs
- Switch between two docs
- Search for a term
- Simulate resize (send WindowSizeMsg) and verify reflow occurs

Assertions:
- Rendered output changes with width.
- Search navigation finds matches.

Acceptance:
- Catches regressions in glamour integration and responsive layout.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:39.583157581Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:35.534673474Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-miqo","depends_on_id":"bd-2lkz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:34.234331367Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-miqo","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:32.891451084Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-miqo","depends_on_id":"bd-39bb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:35.534642717Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-miqo","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:39.598959258Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
